summary: Completed e2e testing for Class Management System. Backend: all 30 pytest tests PASSED (auth, admin CRUD for subjects/users, role guards, teacher attendance/marks, student read-only, cascade delete, data isolation). Frontend: Admin signup works and lands on admin dashboard; subject creation works (counter updated from 0→1 with success toast 'Admin account created!'). Teacher/student creation dialogs open but UI automation couldn't complete form fill due to generic selector ambiguity; admin UI appears functional. Headings use DM Serif Display as requested, pastel neo-brutalist theme applied.

issues: Success toast 'Admin account created!' partially overlaps the Logout button in the top-right corner; consider shifting toast lower or logout left

action_items: Add data-testid attributes to Add Teacher / Add Student dialog form fields (name, email, password, subject-code etc.) so UI automation can target them reliably. Current selectors like [data-testid='teacher-save-btn'] exist but input fields rely on placeholder-based lookup.
Optional: adjust toast placement so it does not overlay the Logout button on admin dashboard.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS is configured with allow_origins=['*'] AND allow_credentials=True which is invalid per CORS spec and breaks credentialed requests in strict browsers; use explicit origin (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) when allow_credentials=True.
JWT_SECRET has insecure default fallback ('dev-change-me-super-secret-...'); production should fail fast if env missing.
No brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (auth playbook requires 5-fail lockout); consider adding rate limiting or attempt tracking.
Open admin signup (/api/auth/register) is intentional per spec but is a security risk in production; consider guarding behind an invite code or disabling once a super-admin exists.
server.py is a single 452-line file; as features grow, split into routers/auth.py, routers/admin.py, routers/teacher.py, routers/student.py.

No unique index on (student_id,subject_id) for enrollments or (teacher_id,subject_id) for teacher_assignments; duplicate assignments possible if admin POSTs same subject twice on update path (currently mitigated by delete_many before insert but not enforced at DB level).

success_rate.frontend: ~70% (signup+admin subject creation verified; teacher/student add not verified via automation)

test_credentials: Admin created per test run with randomized email admin_ui_<id>@example.com / TestPass123 via open signup.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data; each test run creates and cleans up its own users/subjects. A small test admin remains from UI run (not cleaned).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully verified via /app/backend/tests/test_cms_flow.py (pytest, 30 tests). For frontend, add data-testid on input fields inside Add Teacher/Student/Subject dialogs to allow deterministic automation. Dialog save buttons already have testids (teacher-save-btn, student-save-btn, subject-save-btn/subject-name-input if added). Initial 401s on public pages are expected (AuthContext probing /auth/me).
